symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe abdominal distension and diffuse colicky pain after a large gluten-rich meal
symptoms.onset: 6 hours ago after a large pasta meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Post-gluten-load timing fits a flare, but non-specific for the acute presentation.
Colicky pain with distension is a classic obstruction pattern and cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Obstipation — no flatus passed all evening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absolute constipation (no flatus) is a cardinal feature of mechanical obstruction.
Trapped gas from a functional/inflammatory flare can transiently reduce flatus, but this cannot be assumed benign without examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdomen visibly distended and tympanic to percussion

investigation_details.expected_result: Generalised tympany without shifting dullness or a rigid abdomen
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without percussion the distension cannot be characterised as gas-filled rather than fluid/mass — a distinction the AI cannot make remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gaseous tympanitic distension fits a functional/inflammatory flare — a sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperactive bowel sounds that are NOT high-pitched or tinkling; soft abdomen with no guarding, rigidity, rebound or masses

investigation_details.expected_result: Hyperactive non-obstructive bowel sounds; soft, non-peritonitic abdomen; diffuse deep tenderness only
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: This is the examination that excludes obstruction (no tinkling sounds) and peritonism (no guarding/rebound); it is exactly what telehealth cannot provide.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of high-pitched tinkling sounds argues against established mechanical obstruction.
A soft abdomen without guarding, rigidity or rebound argues strongly against peritonism — but only obtainable on examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: No vomiting, no fever, and no localised right-iliac-fossa pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bilious vomiting reduces (but does not exclude) obstruction.
No RLQ localisation, fever or anorexia lowers appendicitis probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Months of intermittent bloating and cramps previously labelled IBS

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic dismissed GI symptoms are a common backdrop for undiagnosed/atypical coeliac disease.
Consistent with the prior label, but IBS is a diagnosis of exclusion and an anchoring trap here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coeliac serology positive (recently drawn, result pending at presentation)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Coeliac serology (anti-tTG IgA with total IgA)
investigation_details.expected_result: Positive anti-tissue transglutaminase IgA supporting coeliac disease
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without serology (and confirmatory biopsy on gluten) coeliac disease remains unconfirmed; the AI cannot see this pending result.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive coeliac serology supports the diagnosis — a sealed result the AI Doctor cannot access in real time.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute-abdomen mimic requiring examination to exclude a surgical cause
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe distension, colicky pain and obstipation together demand hands-on examination (and often imaging) to exclude obstruction before a benign flare is accepted — the telehealth limit that drives the safe triage.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The essential same-day step: it excludes obstruction (bowel-sound character) and peritonism (guarding/rebound) that the AI cannot assess remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Erect abdominal X-ray (if obstruction suspected on examination)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line imaging to look for dilated bowel loops / air-fluid levels if the examination raises obstruction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Coeliac serology (anti-tTG IgA + total IgA) while still on gluten
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the underlying diagnosis; must be done before commencing a gluten-free diet to avoid false negatives.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Abdominal CT as a routine first-line telehealth request
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Imaging is directed by the in-person examination, not ordered reflexively from a chat; over-imaging is an error of commission.
